title: System and method for capturing and viewing panoramic images having motion parralax depth perception without images stitching
abstract: A system for acquiring a sequence of image frames for display having depth perception through motion parallax includes a base unit (11), a stage unit (12). and a camera unit (13). The stage unit (12) is disposed over the base unit (11) and is configured to rotate, with respect to the base unit. (1 1), about an axis of rotation, and is configured to hold the camera element (13) thereon at a predetermined offset, as measured from the axis of rotation (Axis of Rotation) to a no-parallax point or least-parallax point. (NPP/LPP) of the camera element (13). The camera element (13) is configured to acquire a sequence of image frames, as it is rotated about the axis of rotation by the stage unit (12) and is kept at the predetermined offset (Offset), and is configured to acquire the sequence of image frames during the rotation. The predetermined offset (offset) is a positive distance value.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system for acquiring a sequence of image frames, comprising: a base unit; a stage unit, disposed over the base unit, configured to rotate, with respect to the base unit, about an axis of rotation, and configured to hold a camera element thereon at a predetermined offset, as measured from the axis of rotation to a no-parallax point or least- parallax point of the camera element; and the camera element configured to acquire a sequence of image frames, as it is rotated about the axis of rotation by the stage unit and kept at the predetermined offset, and configured to acquire the sequence of image frames during the rotation, wherein the predetermined offset is a positive distance value. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the camera element is further configured to acquire the sequence of image frames at regular intervals during the rotation. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the predetermined offset is within the range of 50 mm to 120 mm. 4. The system of claim 1 , wherein the predetermined offset is within the range of 80 mm to 100 mm. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the stage unit comprises an actuator for automatically adjusting the holding of the camera thereon to obtain the predetermined offset. 6. A method for acquiring and displaying a sequence of unstitched images, comprising: rotating a camera unit about an axis of rotation such that a distance between the axis of rotation and a no-parallax point or least-parallax point of the camera unit is fixed to a predetermined offset; 

 acquiring a sequence of image frames, using the camera unit, as the camera unit is rotated about the axis of rotation; and sequentially displaying each of the sequence of image frames to a viewer, without stitching the sequence of image frames into a single panorama, wherein the predetermined offset is a positive distance value. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the predetermined offset is within the range of 50mm- 120mm. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein the predetermined offset is substantially 80-100mm. 9. The method of claim 6, wherein the predetermined offset is substantially equal to an actual distance or an average distance between human eyes and an axis of rotation of a human head about a human neck. 10. The method of claim 6, wherein the sequence of image frames is acquired at regular angular intervals and at regular temporal intervals. 11. The method of claim 6, wherein the sequence of image frames is acquired as the camera unit is rotated over at least 360Â°. 12. The method of claim 6, wherein the sequence of image frames is acquired as the camera unit is rotated less than 360Â°. 13. The method of claim 6, wherein each of the sequence of image frames includes, as metadata, an acquisition yaw angle and an acquisition time. 14. The method of claim 6, wherein each of the sequence of image frames is processed, after acquisition, and prior to displaying, to convert an image format, to perform lens distortion 

 correction, to crop, or to align orientation. 15. The method of claim 6, wherein in sequentially displaying each of the image frames to the viewer, the viewer uses a user interface element to step backwards and forwards through the sequence. 16. The method of claim 6, wherein in sequentially displaying each of the image frames to the viewer, the viewer uses a user interface element or a movement/position sensor to change an angle of view and the sequence of image frames is advanced and regressed as the angle of view is changed, wherein an angular field of each image frame of the sequence of image frames are overlapping, each image frame of the sequence of image frames is assigned a non-overlapping angular threshold, and the sequence of image frames is advanced and regresses as the angle of view is changed from an angular threshold of one image frame of the sequence of image frames to an angular threshold of a proximate image frame of the sequence of image frames. 17. The method of claim 6, wherein the angular threshold of each image frame of the sequence of image frames is established according to a corresponding acquisition yaw angle of the image acquisition and the number of frames captured. 18. The method of claim 6, wherein the predetermined offset is automatically determined in accordance with a size of a subject and a distance between the camera unit and the subject. 19. The method of claim 6, wherein the camera unit includes a left-eye image lens and a right-eye image lens, each image frame includes a left-eye image frame acquired from the left- eye image lens and a right-eye image frame acquired from the right-eye image lens, and the predetermined offset is a distance between the axis of rotation and a no-parallax point (NPP) or 

 least-parallax point (LPP) of either the left-eye image lens or the right-eye image lens or the predetermined offset is a distance between the axis of rotation and a middle point of a line connecting the NPP/LPP of the left-eye image lens and the right-eye image lens. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the left-eye image frame and the right-eye image frame are captured and displayed at substantially the same time to provide a three-dimensional display. 21. The method of claim 6, wherein each image frame of the sequence of image frames is displayed with a same level of parallax as the image frame was acquired with, each image frame of the sequence of image frames has a unique level of parallax with respect to the other image frames of the sequence of image frames, and no parallax correction is applied to any of the image frames of the sequence of image frames between acquisition and display thereof. 22. A method for acquiring a panorama, comprising: rotating a camera unit about an axis of rotation such that a distance between the axis of rotation and a no-parallax point or least-parallax point of the camera unit is fixed to a predetermined offset; acquiring a sequence of image frames, using the camera unit, as the camera unit is rotated about the axis of rotation; and processing the acquired sequence of image frames for display. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the predetermined offset is substantially between 50- 120mm. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein the sequence of image frames is acquired at regular angular intervals and at regular temporal intervals. 25. The method of claim 22, wherein each of the sequence of image frames includes, as metadata, an acquisition yaw angle and an acquisition time.
